matches.snippet: the letter making of the explots of Fjorgn Camhelsson when he was in the Kvinnes country
matches.reasoning: Direct reference to the hero in 'Finnegan' within the Fjorgn variant name (Fjorgn sounds like 'Fionn'; Camhelsson evokes 'mac Cumhaill')

matches.snippet: leaving by him to oceanic society in his old man without a thing, Tulko MacHooley
matches.reasoning: MacHooley as variant of MacCool/Mac Cumhaill, similar patronymic structure. Associated with 'old man' figure recalling the sleeping giant

matches.snippet: What secondtonone myther rector and maximost bridgesmaker was the first to rise taller
matches.reasoning: Figure who 'rose taller' than giant trees (bluegum, Wellingtonia), establishing the physical enormity/landscape motif of the sleeping giant

matches.snippet: first to rise taller through his beanstale than the bluegum buaboababbaun
matches.reasoning: Evokes Jack's beanstalk and also 'bean' (grain), suggesting the fertility/growth myth associated with Finn; Finn as oversized agricultural figure

matches.snippet: was well known to claud a conciliation cap onto the esker of his booth
matches.reasoning: Esker is a geological ridge formation in Irish landscape; placing it on his booth/head suggests the body-as-landscape motif of Finn recumbent over Dublin

matches.matched_text: thought he weighed a new ton when there felled his first lapapple
matches.snippet: thought he weighed a new ton when there felled his first lapapple
matches.reasoning: Apple-eating recalls the Salmon of Knowledge mythology (Finn gained wisdom from a magical salmon); lapapple suggests both apple and the thumb of wisdom

matches.matched_text: pumped the catholick wartrey and shocked the prodestung boyne
matches.snippet: pumped the catholick wartrey and shocked the prodestung boyne
matches.reasoning: The Boyne (Battle of the Boyne) is historically Irish; 'shocked the boyne' may evoke the watery threshold in Finn mythology; 'wartrey' suggests warfare and water/trey

matches.matched_text: walked as far as the Head where he sat in state as the Rump
matches.snippet: walked as far as the Head where he sat in state as the Rump
matches.reasoning: Howth=Head (Finn's head in Wake geography); the body extended from head to rump over Dublin terrain strongly echoes Finn's recumbent form

matches.matched_text: shows Early English tracemarks and a marigold window
matches.snippet: shows Early English tracemarks and a marigold window with manigilt lights
matches.reasoning: Architectural details (marigold window, ambries, arches) describe the body-as-monument/building, conflating the giant HCE-Finn figure with ecclesiastical structure

matches.matched_text: horologe unstoppable and the Benn of all bells
matches.snippet: is a horologe unstoppable and the Benn of all bells
matches.reasoning: 'Benn' evokes 'ben' (Irish for woman/peak) but also suggests the eternal measuring of time; Finn as cosmic timekeeper and source of all resonance

matches.matched_text: hidal, in carucates he is enumerated, hold as an earl, he counts
matches.snippet: hidal, in carucates he is enumerated, hold as an earl, he counts
matches.reasoning: Carucates are land measures; the figure is measured in terms of territory, reinforcing Finn-as-landscape whose body is quantified in land divisions

matches.matched_text: gave his mundyfoot to Miserius, her pinch to Anna Livia
matches.snippet: gave his mundyfoot to Miserius, her pinch to Anna Livia
matches.reasoning: 'mundyfoot' puns on 'foot' (Finn's foot at Phoenix Park) and 'Mundy'; Anna Livia is Liffey (the river flowing past Finn's recumbent body)

matches.matched_text: cleared out three hundred sixty five idles to set up one all khalassal
matches.snippet: cleared out three hundred sixty five idles to set up one all khalassal for henwives hoping to have males
matches.reasoning: The cyclical reference (365 days/year) suggests temporal resurrection and renewal; clearing and restoration evoke Finnegan's wake and reviving fall

matches.matched_text: the flawhoolagh, the grasping one, the kindler of paschal fire
matches.snippet: the flawhoolagh, the grasping one, the kindler of paschal fire
matches.reasoning: 'flawhoolagh' echoes 'flaming' and 'whoo' (hawk); 'kindler of paschal fire' suggests the Fenian Finn and the sacred fire-keeper role; resurrection through fire

matches.matched_text: the phoenix be his pyre, the cineres his sire
matches.snippet: the phoenix be his pyre, the cineres his sire
matches.reasoning: Phoenix mythology directly parallels Finnegan's fall and resurrection; 'pyre' evokes both cremation and the whiskey/fire that revives the corpse in the ballad

matches.matched_text: piles big pelium on little ossas like the pilluls of hirculeads
matches.snippet: piles big pelium on little ossas like the pilluls of hirculeads
matches.reasoning: Pelion/Ossa reference to giant figures stacking mountains; suggests the colossal body-as-terrain and Finn as a figure of epic proportions built from landscape
